failedAssertions.reason: End-to-end flow blocked: VM guest fails to boot. Kernel boots successfully, rootfs mounts, and /bin/init exists with correct permissions (mode 0755, #!/bin/sh shebang). However, kernel still reports 'Requested init /bin/init failed (error -2)' when trying to execute init. This suggests the init process may be failing to load the shell interpreter or there's a filesystem execution issue.

VM guest fails to boot - init execution fails with error -2 (ENOENT). Concurrent request collapsing mechanism was previously verified to work correctly (two simultaneous requests collapsed to one VM), but end-to-end test cannot complete due to VM health issue.

appliedUpdates.description: Changed guestInitScript from writeShellScript to writeTextFile with explicit #!/bin/sh shebang to avoid Nix store path dependency in guest
Updated VAL-GATEWAY-001 and VAL-VM-004 with new root cause analysis - init exists but execution fails

fixedFromRound3: Guest rootfs now has /bin/init with correct #!/bin/sh shebang (was missing entirely in Round 3)
Guest rootfs includes /bin/sh interpreter for init script
remainingBlocker: Kernel still reports 'Requested init /bin/init failed (error -2)' despite file existing
Init execution fails - possible causes: shared library deps, debugfs file permissions not applied correctly, or ext4 filesystem issue
verifiedWorking: All gateway HTTP assertions (VAL-GATEWAY-002 through 008 from prior rounds)
All VM ownership/registry assertions (VAL-VM-001, 002, 003, 005, 006, 008, 009, 010, 012 from prior rounds)
All cross-area assertions (VAL-CROSS-110, 112-117, 123 from prior rounds)

rootCauseAnalysis.currentIssue: Guest init execution fails despite file existing
symptoms: /bin/init exists with correct shebang (#!/bin/sh) and permissions (0755)
Error -2 is ENOENT (No such file or directory)
possibleCauses: Debugfs modify_inode commands may not be properly setting permissions in ext4 filesystem
The init script may have dependencies on shared libraries not present in guest
The #!/bin/sh shebang may not be resolving correctly
There may be a filesystem corruption or inode issue from debugfs population
The ext4 rootfs created by debugfs may have execution permission issues
nextSteps: Investigate using mkfs.ext4 + mount approach instead of debugfs for rootfs creation
Verify debugfs modify_inode is actually setting permissions correctly
Test init script execution in a chroot or container environment
Check if /bin/sh has any shared library dependencies (ldd) that may be missing
Consider using a simpler init binary instead of shell script

notes: Round 4: Fixed guest rootfs to include /bin/init with portable shebang
Round 4: Guest init exists but kernel still can't execute it - deeper filesystem issue suspected
Flow validator subagent tests were spawned but both reported blocked/failed due to VM boot issue
2 of 2 retested assertions remain blocked by guest VM boot issue
The concurrent request collapsing mechanism was verified in Round 3 and should still work once VM boots
